cases.focus: request defaults, filter defaults, HEAD redirection override, hooks, SSL verify path, and response filter
invalid URL and external-blocked URL return WP_Error and emit debug actions
stream filename default, blocking force, writable directory validation, and filename option handoff
header string parsing, cookie normalization, sslverify=false, body data_format, redirects, and max_bytes
proxy constants, proxy authentication, and SSL verification options are handed to Requests options
Requests exception conversion and nonblocking response shape after dispatch

side_effect_policy.runtime_stubs: Requests classes, selected WordPress globals, hook dispatch, URL validation, temp-dir writability, mbstring encoding guards, and option/bloginfo helpers are deterministic stubs. The oracle root executes copied WordPress WP_Http; the candidate root executes the WPHX PHP compiler-emitted original-path WP_Http request shell. Outbound Requests::request records options or throws without network I/O.

remaining_gaps.detail: This gate records WP_Http's Requests handoff and exception conversion through a stubbed Requests::request boundary. It does not perform live network I/O, DNS, TLS handshake, proxy negotiation, redirect following by Requests, curl/streams execution, timeout races, socket behavior, or real streamed file transfers.
The fixture uses PHP CLI with deterministic support stubs rather than an installed WordPress distribution or ecosystem HTTP callers.

The candidate uses the WPHX PHP compiler-emitted original-path WP_Http request shell and compiled Haxe helper support, but this gate does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership, the full WordPress HTTP API, or installed ecosystem behavior.
